channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Some New York prison guards charged in beating death of handcuffed inmate appear in court
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Six New York prison guards have been indicted for second-degree murder in the beating death of a handcuffed inmate, a brutal incident captured on body-worn cameras that triggered widespread outrage and calls for justice.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: IRS layoffs could hurt revenue collection and foil efforts to go after rich tax dodgers, experts say
item.description: Experts say the layoffs of roughly 7,000 IRS probationary workers likely mean the end of the agency’s plans to go after high-wealth tax dodgers and could spell disaster for revenue collections.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate ready to stay up all night to pass GOP budget over objections from Democrats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senators are ready to stay up all night, launching a budget “vote-a-rama” late Thursday in a crucial, if dreaded, step toward unleashing a $340 billion package President Donald Trump's team says it needs for mass deportations and security measures that top the Republican agenda.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: That marks a break from President Trump and tech billionaire and Trump adviser Elon Musk, who have floated the idea in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Kids' disability rights cases stalled as Trump began to overhaul Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It was obvious to Christine Smith Olsey that her son was not doing well at school, despite educators telling her to leave it to the experts. The second-grade student stumbled over words, and other kids teased him so much he started to call himself “an idiot.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, COLLIN BINKLEY and ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The man accused of stabbing Salman Rushdie declines to take the stand as the defense rests
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — The New Jersey man on trial in the 2022 stabbing of author Salman Rushdie declined to testify in his defense Thursday as his lawyers rested their case without calling any witnesses.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) threw cold water on the idea of using savings from the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) government-slashing activities to send checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KRON4

item.title: Dr. Mehmet Oz holds millions from companies that he'd wield power over if confirmed, report shows
item.description: Dr. Mehmet Oz holds millions of dollars worth of shares in health insurance, fertility, pharmaceutical and vitamin companies.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz And Brian Slodysko, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Latest: Judge declines to halt Trump administration's federal workers layoffs
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday refused to temporarily block the Trump administration’s mass layoff of federal workers while a lawsuit brought by five unions moves forward. U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New arrivals at Guantanamo Bay are Venezuelan immigrants with final deportation orders
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — U.S. immigration and military authorities say they have transferred exclusively Venezuelan immigrants who are subject to final deportation orders to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USS Harry S. Truman commanding officer relieved after collision with merchant ship near Suez Canal
item.description: The commanding officer of the U.S. Navy aircraft carrier USS Harry S. Truman was relieved of his duty on Thursday, just over a week after the vessel collided with a merchant ship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at $4B in funding for California high-speed rail
item.description: The Trump administration is investigating delays and cost overruns in California’s high-speed rail project.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump media company sues a Brazilian Supreme Court justice investigating Bolsonaro
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — President Donald Trump's media company sued a Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Wednesday, accusing him of violating U.S. free speech protections when he imposed a ban on a right-wing supporter of the country's former President Jair Bolsonaro.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: A flight to a Toronto paramedics conference becomes a real emergency for a former EMT
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former paramedic Peter Carlson is still having difficulty putting into perspective the shuddering jolt he and 79 others aboard Delta Air Lines flight 4819 experienced earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: IRS lays off 6,700 employees, torpedoing Democratic enforcement boost
item.description: The IRS fired 6,700 employees Thursday, a government official told NewsNation, the sister television network of The Hill. The employees were designated as probationary, meaning they were working for the agency on a trial basis prior to becoming full staff members.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, WGN 9

item.title: Trump moves hamper bird flu response as egg prices spike
item.description: When President Trump took office, his administration instituted an external communications blackout across health agencies.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Creative control of James Bond franchise given to Amazon MGM
item.description: Amazon MGM Studios and longtime Bond custodians Michael G. Wilson and Barbara Broccoli formed a new joint venture in which they will co-own James Bond intellectual property rights — but Amazon MGM will have creative control.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month into second term: Survey
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating appears to be underwater as many suggest he may be overstepping his authority, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: What's the key to surviving a plane crash? Skilled crew, strong seats and wings that detach
item.description: After watching videos of a Delta Air Lines jet catch fire upon landing and flip over on a Toronto runway, it's fair to wonder how anyone could have survived.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Russian aircraft fly in Alaskan Air Defense Identification Zone, US says
item.description: Russian military aircraft were spotted flying in the Alaskan Air Defense Identification Zone (ADIZ) on Tuesday and Wednesday, U.S. officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump moves hamper bird flu response as egg prices spike
item.description: When President Trump took office, his administration instituted an external communications blackout across health agencies. State and local health departments are only just beginning to hear from officials at the CDC, nearly a month after the inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California sheriff uses 'To Catch A Predator' playbook to squash blue state crime
item.description: A veteran California sheriff and governor hopeful is highlighting one of his biggest career wins happening through a partnership with 'To Catch a Predator' in a massive 2006 operation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Winter-weary East Coast hit with another storm as temperatures plunge elsewhere
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Officials urged people to stay off the roads Wednesday in portions of Virginia and North Carolina where a storm dropped heavy snow and caused hundreds of accidents in places unaccustomed to significant accumulations.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pentagon announces coordinated border patrols with Mexican military amid push for immigration control
item.description: U.S. officials announced on Wednesday that the Mexican military has agreed to coordinated patrols on both sides of the border, as President Trump continues implementing his border plan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New, cheaper iPhone 16e has 'best battery life ever,' Apple says
item.description: "iPhone 16e has the best battery life ever on a 6.1-inch iPhone, lasting up to six hours longer than iPhone 11 and up to 12 hours longer than all generations of iPhone SE," Apple said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Family of DC plane crash victim files $250M claims against FAA, Army
item.description: The claims were filed on behalf of the widow and children of Casey Crafton, 40, of Salem. He died as a result of the crash, leaving behind his wife of 16 years, Rachel, and three young sons.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Schaefer, WGN 9

item.title: Agriculture Department tries to rehire fired workers tied to bird flu response
item.description: The Agriculture Department is scrambling to rehire several workers who were involved in the government's response to the ongoing bird flu outbreak that has devastated egg and poultry farms over the past three years.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: IRS plans to lay off 7,000 probationary workers beginning Thursday, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off roughly 7,000 workers in Washington and around the country beginning Thursday, a person familiar with the plans told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This rookie QB card could be worth $50K if you pull it from a new Topps set
item.description: Denver Broncos rookie quarterback Bo Nix impressed on the field, and now a Colorado sports card store is ready to pay $50,000 to keep a unique card featuring him close to home.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, Fox 5

item.title: Colorado city helps dozens of families relocate after Tren de Aragua apartment infestation
item.description: The Aurora, Colorado police chief said Wednesday that efforts to clean up an apartment complex infested with Venezuelan gang members, involved relocating 85 families.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KTLA 5

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama police department called a 'criminal enterprise' following indictment of 5 officers
item.description: "This is a sad day for law enforcement but a good day for the rule of law," District Attorney Champ Crocker said.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, NewsNation

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month. Here's a look at each of the crashes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
